behavior_summary: U8P_TORTRACK::look is a simple examine/U8P_TORTRACK::look handler for the TORTRACK entity. When the player uses the 'U8P_TORTRACK::look' (examine) action on this object/NPC, the script immediately displays a single short text/bark: "torture rack" and then suspends/ends the U8P_TORTRACK::look process.
There are no dialogue branches, menus, external calls, or global flags affected. The behavior is non-interactive and purely descriptive.
notable_control_flow: Entry: process-type 0x0208 U8P_TORTRACK::look/examine event for TORTRACK.
Action: emit one Bark/DialogueLine containing the string "torture rack".
Termination: suspend/finish the U8P_TORTRACK::look process immediately after emitting the bark (non-interactive).
open_questions: Is TORTRACK an inanimate object (prop) or an immobile NPC? The script's single descriptive bark strongly suggests an object, but the file alone does not conclusively label it.
Are there any engine-side interactions tied to process suspension here (e.g., timing or animation hooks) that are not visible in this disassembly fragment?

main_function.name: U8P_TORTRACK::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's examine/U8P_TORTRACK::look action for TORTRACK by presenting a short descriptive text and ending the U8P_TORTRACK::look process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine when the player 'looks at' or examines the TORTRACK entity.
